spec.reasoning: The task references specific cell addresses (B2:E16) and column letters (F through AT, B through E), so inserting a title row is unsafe as flagged by the risk flag and confirmed by the failed attempt. Instead, I use two safe strategies: (1) Rename columns — 'Staff' becomes 'Employee', and the Meeting Start/Finish columns get abbreviated equivalents (Mtg Begin/End). These are purely column-reference names not used as general English words in the instruction. The sheet is also renamed from 'Edit' to 'Schedule'. (2) Add a distractor column 'Mtg Notes' after the last column, which is plausibly related to meetings but clearly different from the time-slot columns and the start/finish columns. No title row is inserted, preserving the header at row 1 and data starting at row 2, so all cell references in the instruction and answer remain valid. The time-slot headers (08:00, 08:15, etc.) are not renamed since they represent actual time values critical to the formula logic.
